data.title: Citi Sees Cooler September Core Inflation — Market Talk
data.description:
1011 ET - If it continues, the government shutdown may delay the release of official September inflation data, scheduled for Wednesday. Citi economists think that when the data finally materialize, they will show cooler core price increases last month. Citi thinks the core CPI was up 0.28% in September, cooler than the 0.35% figure from August. Core goods price increases likely firmed up under continued pressure from tariffs, per Citi. But cooler shelter inflation should help calm price increases for services, which make up a much bigger portion of the CPI than goods do. Inflation has been stubborn, Citi allows, "but a softening labor market and slowing home prices makes us less concerned that these pressures will be persistent."(matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

1007 ET - John Williams, president of the New York Fed, says that his concerns about the labor market would likely lead him to support further rate cuts this year, in an interview with The New York Times. As a close ally of Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Williams is an influential voice on the committee and his comments confirm that the Fed will seriously weigh further cuts at its two remaining meetings this year. "The risks of a further slowdown in the labor market is something I'm very focused on," Williams says in the interview.(matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

0932 ET - Crude futures move higher after slipping in earlier trade on a deal aimed at ending the Israel-Hamas war and hostage crisis. Oil futures are "in a corrective phase as the Israel/Hamas conflict looks to be ending," Dennis Kissler of BOK Financial says in a note. The large U.S. crude oil stock build reported yesterday was offset by draws in diesel and gasoline inventories, pointing to buoyant fuel demand, he says. "With rising crude inventories, along with stronger fuel demand, crude looks to be locked in a $60-$65/bbl range in the near term." WTI is up 0.3% at $62.74 a barrel, and Brent is 0.2% higher at $66.38.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

0930 ET - A lack of strong storm activity and slow new construction have hurt the roofing sector, say Truist analysts Keith Hughes and Mia Senyitko, who lower estimates for Owens Corning and QXO. There has been below average hail activity year-to-date and no major hurricanes, while new construction activity has weakened. "The result is a market that has seen in our view modest single digit declines at all levels of the value chain," the analysts say. They expect inventory reductions and weak pricing in 4Q25/1Q26. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0921 ET - Russia's agricultural minister says the country is expected to seed 2% less wheat this marketing year than it did in the last one, counter to expectations of more wheat being planted. Russian farmers appear to be choosing to plant more oilseeds, says AgResource in a note, adding that farmers are expected to seed 8.5% more oilseed crops including soybeans, due to higher profit margins versus wheat. CBOT wheat futures are up 0.8% premarket, while corn is down 0.1% and soybeans slip 0.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

0908 ET - Investors have been pulling out of the restaurant sector, hurting Domino's stock, but the pizza restaurant shouldn't be treated like other restaurant stocks, say Oppenheimer analysts Brian Bittner and Michael Tamas. "A sluggish low-end consumer and choppy macro/jobs scenario has created headwinds to industry trends and dramatically reduced investor sentiment," the analysts say, adding that restaurant stocks are down 19% over the last three months. Investors are particularly worried about Domino's sales once its "best-deal-ever" promotion ends, but Domino's new DoorDash partnership and stuffed-crust offerings could create multiple years of incremental growth. "With only about 23% market share today, opportunity to extend share gains has massive runway," the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0856 ET - Treasury yields and the dollar rise slightly as the government shutdown goes on. Weekly jobless claims report is delayed for the second consecutive week. Economists surveyed by WSJ expected 230,000 new claims, which would keep the layoffs gauge within range. The 10-year is at 4.129% and the two-year at 3.591%. The WSJ Dollar Index rises slightly. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0850 ET - Costco is contending with tough-to-beat sales comparisons from the previous year, but its share gains are allowing it to outperform in the current backdrop, say Oppenheimer analysts in a research note. Costco posted a 5% increase in September U.S. comparable sales on top of a 9.3% gain last year that was boosted by abnormal consumer activity associated with Hurricane Helene and port strikes. Very difficult upcoming compares in the non-foods category are still on their way, the analysts say, but the September results inspire "even more confidence in the company's ability to lap challenging comparisons." Costco up 1.3% premarket.(nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0833 ET - PepsiCo's North American beverage unit saw volumes slip 3% during the recent quarter, but those losses were offset by volume gains across the company's Europe, Middle East and Africa beverage unit, as well as its Asia-Pacific food group. The company's namesake cola delivered both volume and net revenue growth during 3Q, driven by increased marketing efforts and strength across varieties including Pepsi Zero Sugar, PepsiCo says. Outside of cola, the company's modern soda category--led by its Poppi brand--is resonating well with consumers and gaining market share, the company says. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0815 ET - PepsiCo, which has recently come under activist pressure to cull its underperforming brands, says it plans to reduce nearly 15% of its SKUs during the current quarter. PepsiCo plans to focus on products that lean into current trends, such as health and wellness. PepsiCo will introduce Doritos Protein, as well as new offerings with fiber, whole grains and protein from brands such as Quaker, Sun Chips, PopCorners and Smartfood, it says. In beverage, PepsiCo will release drinks including Pepsi Prebiotic, while continuing to advance its zero-sugar platforms and cutting out artificial sweeteners, flavors and colors. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

0725 ET - Delta Air Lines is seeing a strong rebound in corporate travel where 3Q sales rose 8%, with sequential improvement across all sectors that's likely to continue. Delta says recent corporate survey results show that roughly 90 percent of companies expect their travel volume to increase or remain steady in 2026, 5 points higher than last year's survey at this time. Delta climbs 5% premarket. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

0353 ET - Valuations on emerging market equities and Chinese equities look more attractive than U.S. valuations, Crédit Mutuel Asset Management's François Rimeu says in a note. U.S. stock valuations are strteched, Rimeu says. "Even with very optimistic profit forecasts [in the U.S.], the upside potential seems quite low to us." Crédit Mutuel Asset Management favors investing in equities, particularly emerging markets and Chinese equities. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Brazil's cenbank director says tightening pause means longer hold on rate levels
data.description:
By Marcela Ayres

Brazil's central bank monetary policy director Nilton David said on Thursday that the decision by policymakers to interrupt the monetary tightening cycle requires keeping interest rates unchanged for longer than if they had continued raising them.

"This decision was made by the committee and it is being applied now, which is why we are in the very prolonged period (of unchanged rates)," he said at an event hosted by the Spanish Chamber of Commerce in Sao Paulo.

Last month, Brazil's central bank kept its benchmark Selic rate steady at 15%, the highest level in nearly 20 years, for a second consecutive meeting, signaling that borrowing costs will remain elevated for an extended period.

According to David, the use of the term "very prolonged" in the bank's official communication basically reflects the trade-off resulting from policymakers' decision not to raise rates beyond 15%.

The central bank's move to halt interest rate hikes without waiting for inflation expectations to decline further was "very well received" by markets, he said.

Still, David noted there is unanimous discomfort within the rate-setting committee over market inflation expectations remaining above the 3% target.

As the central bank works to bring inflation back to its official target in a steady and sustainable way, David said the economy must not grow faster than its long-term capacity. He noted that Brazil is currently experiencing such above-potential growth.

He pointed to the sharp rise in employment and strong income growth in Latin America's largest economy.

David added there seems to be a consensus that the central bank is doing the right thing to guide inflation toward its target, amid what he described as a "well-behaved" exchange rate and lower volatility along the yield curve.

He also noted that the nearly 10% increase in Brazil's foreign exchange reserves this year was due to the profitability of the investments, not to dollar purchases, adding they are valuable asset and remain at a reasonable level.

data.title: CN Rail, Congebec Partner on New Cold-Storage Facility at Alberta Rail Hub in Calgary
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

Canadian National Railway will work with logistics provider Congebec to build a new cold-storage facility at the rail company's Calgary logistics hub in Alberta.

The facility will be designed to speed up the movement of temperature-sensitive goods between rail and warehouse, CN said. Customers will benefit from a more reliable, timely and efficient service to get perishable cargo to domestic and international markets.

The new facility will be built nearer CN's rail line, which the company said will address long-standing challenges in the supply chain by enabling faster container flows, flexible on-demand capacity and more reliable delivery schedules.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: Firms Must Learn to Live With Elevated Uncertainty, Bank of Canada Official Says
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--Canadian businesses must start making investments that offset the hit from President Trump's trade policy or risk being left behind, says the Bank of Canada's No. 2 official.

"There's an enormous amount of uncertainty right now, and I think that's very understandably a difficult environment to invest in. But that is exactly what we need to do," Carolyn Rogers told a blue-chip Toronto audience, following remarks on competition policy. Firms need to figure out how much policy uncertainty they are prepared to live with, she added.

"Don't hold your breath and hide under a table," Rogers said, in a message to companies. "If we don't figure out how to offset that structural change with a positive structural change, we are on a permanently lower [growth] path — which is not what we want."

The Bank of Canada cut its benchmark policy rate last month to help an economy shaken by Trump's trade policy, which has imposed hefty tariffs on key Canadian sectors like steel, aluminum, automobiles and forest products. Economic output contracted in the second quarter, due to a plunge in exports, and the unemployment rate has climbed to a nine-year high, excluding the Covid-19 pandemic period, as companies in trade-exposed sector reduce their payrolls.

Prime Minister Mark Carney met with Trump in Washington this week, and senior officials from both countries are working toward deals on steel, aluminum and energy, the Canadian leader said. The Canadian government's annual budget plan, out Nov. 4, is set to include spending and other measures aimed at lifting business investment — which in the second quarter fell to a historic low.

Canadian officials said this week that increasing business investment is near the top of policy priorities, after years of lackluster spending on machinery, equipment and software. Carney's policy agenda includes spurring the building on infrastructure and energy projects that can help rewire the Canadian economy and make it less reliant on a protectionist U.S.

Rogers said Canadian companies and policymakers have been complacent about underlying structural problems with the domestic economy, notably moribund productivity and weak business investment. She said the country had relied too heavily on selling goods, such as commodities like energy and minerals, to the U.S. tariff-free under the provisions of a North American trade treaty. She said tariff-free trade with the U.S. is not coming back.

Rogers told the audience of a recent conversation with a software entrepreneur who developed an education platform, which she argued epitomized Canada's complacency.

"She said when she [tries] to sell the platform in Canada, the first question she often gets is, 'Has anybody in the U.S. bought it yet?' This sort of seems like a backwards question to me," Rogers said.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Figma Expands Partnership With Google to Bring Generative AI to Designers
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Figma and Google Cloud said they are expanding their partnership to integrate more of Google's generative artificial-intelligence technology into Figma's design and product-development tools.

Under the collaboration, Figma will leverage Google's Gemini AI models--including Gemini 2.5 Flash, Gemini 2.0 and Imagen 4--to power image generation and editing across its platform. The company said Thursday the integration will let its 13 million monthly active users create and refine visuals more quickly, as well as experiment with product ideas inside Figma.

Figma said early tests of Gemini 2.5 Flash made its "Make Image" feature about twice as fast, allowing users to generate and edit visuals with less delay. The company said it will also rely on Google Cloud's AI systems and cybersecurity tools to support its growing lineup of AI features.

"Our collaboration with Google Cloud brings powerful image generation and editing capabilities into Figma that help teams tap into their creativity without breaking their flow," Chief Executive Dylan Field said.

The move comes as design-software makers such as Adobe and Canva race to embed generative AI into creative workflows to speed up production and reduce manual design tasks.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: American Water to Discuss Third Quarter 2025 Earnings and Initiation of 2026 Earnings Guidance on October 30, 2025
data.description:
CAMDEN, N.J.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--October 09, 2025--

American Water Works Company, Inc. announced today that it intends to release its 2025 third quarter financial results, 2026 earnings guidance and long-term financial targets after the market closes on Wednesday, October 29, 2025.

John Griffith, President and Chief Executive Officer; David Bowler, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer; and Cheryl Norton, Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer, will host a conference call and webcast with investors, analysts and other interested parties on Thursday, October 30, 2025, at 9 a.m. Eastern Time. There will be a question-and-answer session as part of the call.

Interested parties may listen to an audio webcast of the conference call through a link on the Investor Relations website at ir.amwater.com. Presentation slides that will be used in conjunction with the earnings conference call will also be made available online in advance at ir.amwater.com. A replay of the audio webcast will be available for one year on American Water's investor relations website at ir.amwater.com/events. The company recognizes its website as a key channel of distribution to reach public investors and as a means of disclosing material non-public information to comply with its obligations under SEC Regulation FD.

About American Water

American Water is the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States. With a history dating back to 1886, We Keep Life Flowing(R) by providing safe, clean, reliable and affordable drinking water and wastewater services to more than 14 million people with regulated operations in 14 states and on 18 military installations. American Water's 6,700 talented professionals leverage their significant expertise and the company's national size and scale to achieve excellent outcomes for the benefit of customers, employees, investors and other stakeholders.

For more information, visit amwater.com and join American Water on LinkedIn, Facebook, X and Instagram.

data.description:
** J.P.Morgan cuts aircraft parts maker TransDigm's PT to $1405 from $1500, representing 9.3% upside on stock's last close

** TransDigm shares fall nearly 1% to about $1278.5 in early trading

** "TDG has a model for generating equity returns by focusing on the aftermarket, pricing its products aggressively, undertaking accretive acquisitions, and employing financial leverage," brokerage says

** Says co's underperformance since its last earnings report is notable, and brokerage has been relatively cautious on stock

** 18 of 22 brokerages rate stock "buy" or higher and four "hold"; their median PT is $1550 - data compiled by LSEG

** Including session moves, shares up about 9% YTD

data.title: terminates share repurchase programme
data.description:
Frequentis AG

by Stefan Marin

Purchase of own shares according to article 5 MAR / art. 2 Regulation (EU) 2016/1052

Frequentis AG: terminates share repurchase programme

Vienna, Austria (pta000/09.10.2025/16:07 UTC+2)

On 8 September 2025, the Executive Board of Frequentis AG resolved to carry out a share repurchase programme pursuant to Section 65 Para 1 No. 4 and 8 of the Austrian Stock Corporation Act (AktG) based on the existing authorization of the General Meeting dated 6 June 2024. Such resolution and further details on the repurchase programme have been publicly disclosed on 8 September 2025 in accordance with Article 17 of the Regulation (EU) 596/2014.

The Executive Board of Frequentis AG hereby gives notice that the repurchase programme was terminated as planned on 8 October 2025.

Summary of the repurchase programme:

Total volume repurchased6,000 shares
Percentage of share capitalapprox. 0.05%
Highest price per shareEUR 82.00
Lowest price per shareEUR 58.00
Weighted average price per shareEUR 66.2629
Total value of shares repurchasedEUR 397,577.18
Termination of repurchase programme8 October 2025

Details on the individual transactions of the share repurchase can be found on the website at www.frequentis.com > Investor Relations > Share > Share Repurchase 2025.

About FREQUENTIS

Frequentis stands for a safer world. Our solutions are used in our customers' command and control centres to help them make the world safer.

Frequentis is a world leader in high-tech solutions for Air Traffic Management, supporting both civil and military air traffic control organisations, as well as Public Safety and Transport, where police, emergency rescue services, fire brigades, railways, coastguards, and port authorities rely on our extensive portfolio.

The listed family business based in Vienna, Austria, drives innovative and sustainable solutions for safety and security in everyday life and in the safety-critical sector. Its air traffic optimisation solutions for air traffic control centres are contributing to reducing emissions.

As a global player with more than 2,500 employees (full-time equivalents/FTE), Frequentis has a worldwide network of companies in over 50 countries. Its products, services, and solutions are used in around 150 countries. Shares in Frequentis are traded on the Vienna and Frankfurt stock exchanges; ISIN: ATFREQUENT09, WKN: A2PHG5. In 2024, revenues were EUR 480.3 million and EBIT was EUR 32.1 million.

For more information, please visit www.frequentis.com
